question_text: Two identical metal spheres both have a negative charge. Sphere A has a charge of -8 units and Sphere B has a charge of -3 units. What type of force will act between them?

options.label: A
options.text: Attractive force, because both are negatively charged

options.label: B
options.text: Repulsive force, because both spheres have the same type of charge

options.label: C
options.text: No force, because the charges are different amounts

options.label: D
options.text: Attractive force, because negative charges are stronger

explanation: Like charges always repel each other. Since both spheres are negatively charged, they will push away from each other. The magnitude of the charge doesn't matter—only that the charges are the same type.
